segments.text: Lord, you are the God who saves me; day and night I cry out to you.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: May my prayer come before you; turn your ear to my cry.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: I am overwhelmed with troubles and my life draws near to death.
I am counted among those who go down to the pit; I am like one without strength.
I am set apart with the dead, like the slain who lie in the grave, whom you remember no more, who are cut off from your care.
You have put me in the lowest pit, in the darkest depths.
Your wrath lies heavily on me; you have overwhelmed me with all your waves.
You have taken from me my closest friends and have made me repulsive to them. I am confined and cannot escape;
my eyes are dim with grief. I call to you, Lord, every day; I spread out my hands to you.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Do you show your wonders to the dead? Do their spirits rise up and praise you?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Is your love declared in the grave, your faithfulness in Destruction?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Are your wonders known in the place of darkness, or your righteous deeds in the land of oblivion?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: But I cry to you for help, Lord; in the morning my prayer comes before you.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Why, Lord, do you reject me and hide your face from me?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: From my youth I have suffered and been close to death; I have borne your terrors and am in despair.
Your wrath has swept over me; your terrors have destroyed me.
All day long they surround me like a flood; they have completely engulfed me.
You have taken from me friend and neighbor— darkness is my closest friend.
